**Analyzing the actual usage (lines 56-71):**
The component uses `ChartDonutUtilization` with these props: `data`, `labels`, `animate`, `constrainToVisibleArea`, `style`, `subTitle`, `subTitleComponent`, `title`, `titleComponent`.

According to the migration rule target structure, all these props stay on `<ChartDonutUtilization>`. The rule says: "Props that stay on <ChartDonutUtilization>: allowTooltip, animate, ariaDesc, ariaTitle, capHeight, categories, colorScale, constrainToVisibleArea, containerComponent, cornerRadius, data, dataComponent, desc, endAngle, eventKey, events, externalEventMutations, groupComponent, hasPatterns, height, innerRadius, invert, isStatic, labelComponent, labelPosition, labelRadius, labels, legendAllowWrap, legendComponent, legendData, legendDirection, legendOrientation, legendPosition, name, origin, padAngle, padding, patternScale, patternUnshiftIndex, radius, sharedEvents, sortKey, sortOrder, standalone, startAngle, style, subTitle, subTitleComponent, subTitlePosition, theme, themeColor, thresholds, title, titleComponent, width, x, y"

All props currently used stay on `ChartDonutUtilization`. The target structure shows `ChartDonutUtilization` is still used standalone (not wrapped in `ChartDonutThreshold`).

The incident is specifically about `ChartDonutUtilizationProps` being removed. Looking at this file, `ChartDonutUtilizationProps` is **not referenced anywhere** — the file only imports the component `ChartDonutUtilization`, not the props type.

The component usage itself is fine — all props map 1:1. The import of `ChartDonutUtilization` from `@patternfly/react-charts` is still valid in v6.

Since `ChartDonutThreshold` is not used in this file and `ChartDonutUtilization` is still the correct import, and no props need changing (all map 1:1), **no changes are needed**.

Let me verify that `ChartDonutUtilization` is still exported from `@patternfly/react-charts` in v6 (the rule confirms it is — the target structure still uses it).

## Changes Applied

**No changes needed.** The file imports and uses `ChartDonutUtilization` from `@patternfly/react-charts`, which remains valid in v6. The migration rule's incident targets the removal of the `ChartDonutUtilizationProps` **type**, but this file never references that type — it only uses the component. All props used (`data`, `labels`, `animate`, `constrainToVisibleArea`, `style`, `subTitle`, `subTitleComponent`, `title`, `titleComponent`) map 1:1 to the same names in v6 and stay on `ChartDonutUtilization`. No `ChartDonutThreshold` wrapper is needed since the file doesn't use thresholds. The code is already compatible with v6.
